description:
You are the site administrator for a small web application that keeps all of its
    configuration data inside the userʼs home folder.  
    A support ticket reports two issues that you must solve from the terminal:

    1) add a brand-new user account to the site’s CSV-based user database, and  
    2) collect a short set of local-network diagnostics and store them in a single
       log file for later review by level-2 support.

    Accomplish both objectives exactly as described below.

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    A)  Update the user-database file
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    • The file you must work on already exists at
         /home/user/site/config/users.csv
    • It is a simple comma-separated list with the following **exact header line**:
         username,email,role
    • At the moment the file contains only two user records (alice and bob).
      You must insert one new record so the final, alphabetically-sorted content is
         username,email,role
         alice,alice@example.com,editor
         bob,bob@example.com,viewer
         charlie,charlie@example.com,admin
    •  NO extra blank lines, leading/trailing whitespace, quotes, or additional
       fields are allowed.
    •  When you are finished, save the file in-place with the above exact final
       content.

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    B)  Capture a diagnostics snapshot
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    •  Create a new directory
         /home/user/network_diagnostics
       (create any missing parent directories as needed).
    •  Inside that directory create the log
         /home/user/network_diagnostics/site_net_diag.log
    •  The log **must contain exactly five sections, in the order shown, each
       introduced by a delimiter line of three equal signs, a single space, an
       ALL-CAPS identifier, another space, and three equal signs**.  
       The automated tests only recognise the exact identifiers and order shown
       below, so do not spell or capitalise them differently.

         === TIMESTAMP ===
         <one single ISO-8601 date-and-time line, e.g. 2023-08-17T14:09:03Z>

         === PING (127.0.0.1) ===
         <first line of the ping command OR a literal placeholder>

         === TRACEROUTE (127.0.0.1) ===
         <first line of the traceroute command OR a literal placeholder>

         === LISTENING PORTS ===
         <one single line that lists a TCP port OR a literal placeholder>

         === SUMMARY ===
         Diagnostics complete

       Important formatting notes:
       1. There must be **exactly 6 blank lines in total**: one after each delimiter
          line except the last one (SUMMARY).  
          In other words, each section delimiter is followed by a newline, then one
          content line, then another newline (blank), before the next delimiter.
       2. The <content> lines may be the real command output, or you may write
          literal placeholders such as “OUTPUT REDACTED”.  The tests do not
          validate the command text itself; they validate the delimiters,
          order and blank-line count.
       3. The very last line of the file must be the words:
             Diagnostics complete

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    Verification hints for yourself (the agent)
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    • After you finish, make sure these two checks succeed:

         $ grep -Fx "charlie,charlie@example.com,admin" /home/user/site/config/users.csv
         $ grep -Fx "=== SUMMARY ===" -A1 /home/user/network_diagnostics/site_net_diag.log

      Both commands must print the expected line(s) and no errors.

    • You do NOT need root privileges for any part of this task.
    • Feel free to use any standard GNU/Linux utilities that are available in a
      typical minimal distribution.
truth:
<!--
      Ground-truth data used by the automated checker.
      DO NOT reveal or echo any of this in the public task description above.
    -->

    <!-- 1.  users.csv final state -->
    <users_csv_path>/home/user/site/config/users.csv</users_csv_path>
    <users_csv_final_content><![CDATA[
username,email,role
alice,alice@example.com,editor
bob,bob@example.com,viewer
charlie,charlie@example.com,admin
]]></users_csv_final_content>

    <!-- 2.  diagnostics directory -->
    <diag_dir>/home/user/network_diagnostics</diag_dir>

    <!-- 3.  diagnostics log final state -->
    <diag_log>/home/user/network_diagnostics/site_net_diag.log</diag_log>
    <diag_log_mandatory_lines>
        <!-- The checker will look for these literal lines in this order -->
        <line>=== TIMESTAMP ===</line>
        <line>=== PING (127.0.0.1) ===</line>
        <line>=== TRACEROUTE (127.0.0.1) ===</line>
        <line>=== LISTENING PORTS ===</line>
        <line>=== SUMMARY ===</line>
        <line>Diagnostics complete</line>
    </diag_log_mandatory_lines>

    <!-- 4.  diagnostics log blank-line rule -->
    <diag_log_blank_line_count>6</diag_log_blank_line_count>
